title: Beehiiv vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Beehiiv and GetResponse. See pricing, features, and which email marketing platform suits your needs best.
intro: Both Beehiiv and GetResponse are popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Beehiiv focuses on newsletter creators and publishers, while GetResponse caters to online course creators and webinar hosts. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right tool for your business.

comparison_table.tool_b: Online course creators and webinar hosts

choose_a_if: You're a newsletter publisher looking to monetize your audience through ads and sponsorships
You want a platform specifically optimized for newsletter growth with built-in referral programs
You prefer a simpler, more focused tool dedicated to email and newsletter content
choose_b_if: You need webinar hosting, online courses, and landing pages alongside email marketing
You want a more affordable starting price and comprehensive all-in-one marketing platform
You're creating conversion funnels and need advanced automation and sales capabilities

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your use case. Beehiiv excels for newsletter creators with monetization features, while GetResponse offers broader functionality including webinars, courses, and landing pages. Beehiiv has higher user ratings (4.7 vs 4.2 on G2), but GetResponse provides more tools in one platform.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Beehiiv or GetResponse?
faq.a: GetResponse is cheaper overall, starting at $19/month compared to Beehiiv's $39/month. However, Beehiiv's free tier supports 2,500 subscribers versus GetResponse's 500, making Beehiiv better value if you're bootstrapping a newsletter with a larger audience.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to Beehiiv?
faq.a: Yes, you can switch between platforms. Both allow you to export your subscriber lists and email content, though you may need to manually recreate some automations and designs. Plan your migration carefully to avoid losing data or downtime.
